title: Forward embargoed CV as the model-selection gate
summary: Current grouped CV can still look optimistic for a next-lap event because adjacent laps from the same race regime remain highly correlated.
rationale: Current grouped CV can still look optimistic for a next-lap event because adjacent laps from the same race regime remain highly correlated. Purged/embargoed validation is a standard fix in event-driven domains, and multiple-testing literature suggests small CV gains are easy to overtrust when trying many blends.
implementation_hint: Keep training rows sorted by season-race-lap. Build 5-fold forward splits by race chronology, then embargo 1-2 laps (or one full race block) around each validation fold; use mean-minus-std AUC across those splits to choose features/blends, while keeping the existing CV only as a secondary diagnostic.
expected_effect: May not raise raw local CV, but should reduce false positives and improve the chance that any new gain survives on Kaggle test data.
risk: Can lower apparent CV and reduce train size; if the test set is IID rather than forward-shifted, the stricter split may underfit model selection.
